pattern: The circular maximum is either a standard Kadane max-subarray or total_sum minus a Kadane min-subarray; take the larger, handling the all-negative edge case.
hints: A circular subarray either lives entirely within the linear array, or wraps around the ends.
The wrap-around maximum equals total_sum minus the minimum non-circular subarray sum.
When all elements are negative, the wrap-around formula gives 0 (empty), which is invalid; fall back to the linear Kadane result.

edge_inputs.description: all positive, wrap spans entire array
all negative, must return single max element

stress.description: random integer arrays with both positive and negative values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(30000, max(1, size))
    nums = [rng.randint(-30000, 30000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def maxSubarraySumCircular(self, nums):
        # Kadane for max
        max_sum = cur_max = nums[0]
        # Kadane for min
        min_sum = cur_min = nums[0]
        total = nums[0]
        for x in nums[1:]:
            cur_max = max(x, cur_max + x)
            max_sum = max(max_sum, cur_max)
            cur_min = min(x, cur_min + x)
            min_sum = min(min_sum, cur_min)
            total += x
        # If all negative, max_sum is the answer (total - min_sum would be 0)
        if max_sum < 0:
            return max_sum
        return max(max_sum, total - min_sum)
